channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Partisan divide widening on US support for NATO: Pew
item.description: A partisan divide is widening among Americans on the country's support for NATO, with Democrats being more likely to have a positive opinion of the military alliance than Republicans, according to a Thursday survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Iran seeks Russia's support for its nuclear talks with US
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran on Friday sought support from Russia over a possible deal with Washington over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program, ahead of a second round of talks this weekend in Rome.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-ICE director says Abrego Garcia will be 'deported again' if returned to US
item.description: A retired acting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) director said Kilmar Abrego Garcia, the Maryland man who was deported to the terrorist confinement center (CECOT) in El Salvador, will be removed again if he is approved to return to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Worshippers return to restored Notre Dame for Easter rituals with emotion and awe
item.description: Four months after its reopening, Notre Dame Cathedral hosted an emotional Easter ceremony Friday centered on the Crown of Thorns relic.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nouvian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US lawmakers' bipartisan Taiwan visit signals support despite harsh words and tariffs from Trump
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Republican and Democratic lawmakers made their first trip to Taiwan under the new Trump administration a bipartisan one, aiming to show both Taiwan and China that U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, ELLEN KNICKMEYER and SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venice expands day-tripper tax in bid to combat overtourism
item.description: Venice has started charging day-trippers to the famed canal city an arrivals tax for the second year, in a measure aimed at combating the kind of overtourism that put the city’s UNESCO World Cultural Heritage status at risk.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 25 in Gaza and Huckabee makes first appearance as US ambassador
item.description: Israeli airstrikes across Gaza have killed at least 25 people including children as the new U.S. ambassador to Israel made his first public appearance in Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China’s leader ends Southeast Asia tour touting Beijing's reliability vs. US tariff threats
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping has capped a three-nation Southeast Asian tour in Cambodia by promoting Beijing’s reliability as the region faces U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariffs that threaten its export-oriented economies.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And Grant Peck, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Carney says lower internal trade barriers will help Canada more than Trump's tariffs will harm it
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Mark Carney said Thursday eliminating trade barriers within Canada would benefit Canadians far more than U.S. President Donald Trump can ever take away with his trade war as he made his case to retain power at the last debate ahead of the April 28 vote.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN nuclear watchdog says US-Iran talks at a 'very crucial' stage
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program are “in a very crucial” stage, the head of the United Nations' nuclear watchdog said Thursday while on a visit to the Islamic Republic.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A recovering Pope Francis visits Rome prison to keep annual Holy Week appointment
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis visited Rome's central prison Thursday and met with dozens of inmates as he kept an Easter season appointment to spend Holy Thursday among the least fortunate, even as he continues recovering from a life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thousands of Gaza children are malnourished under Israel's food blockade, aid groups say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Aid groups are raising new alarm over Israel’s blockade of the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where it has barred entry of all food and other goods for more than six weeks.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, FATMA KHALED and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US to withdraw 600 troops from Syria, leaving fewer than 1,000 to help counter IS militants
item.description: The U.S. troops have been critical not only in the operations against the Islamic State but as a buffer for the Kurdish forces against Turkey.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Haitians face record hunger as gang violence grips country in throes of economic crisis
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — More than half of Haiti’s population is expected to experience severe hunger through June, and another 8,400 people living in makeshift shelters are projected to starve, according to a new report released this week.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Van Hollen blocked from visiting mistakenly deported man in El Salvador
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) said he and a lawyer for the family of Kilmar Abrego Garcia were stopped at a security checkpoint in El Salvador on Thursday when they tried to visit the mistakenly deported man at CECOT prison.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis visits Rome prison during Holy Week
item.description: Pope Francis kept annual Holy Week appointment visiting dozens of inmates in a Rome prison on Thursday. The 88-year-old is still recovering from pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump takes center stage in Canada’s prime minister election debate
item.description: Canada's electoral debate for the next prime minister focused on who is best equipped to counter President Trump amid his tariff war and his 51st state comments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former FAA contractor pleads guilty to sharing US airport files with Iran
item.description: A former Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) contractor pleaded guilty Wednesday to working as an agent of Iran and sharing sensitive U.S. airport data with Iranian intelligence officers. Abouzar Rahmati, 42, admitted to conspiring to act and acting as an agent of Iran on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Burchett signals support for sending US criminals to El Salvador
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Wednesday defended President Trump’s suggestion that U.S. citizens convicted of violent crimes be sent to Salvadoran prisons. “They're criminals. They broke our laws.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Israeli troops will remain in Gaza buffer zones indefinitely, Defense Minister Katz says
item.description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz says troops will indefinitely remain in the buffer zone they created in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Portrait of Palestinian boy who lost both arms is World Press Photo of the year for 2025
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A portrait of a young Palestinian boy who lost both arms as a result of an Israeli attack in Gaza was honored Thursday as World Press Photo of the year.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine's Kherson kill 1 person and wound 9
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian glide bombs and artillery struck a city in southern Ukraine on Wednesday, killing one person and wounding nine others as Moscow forces continued daily attacks across the country.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea threatens a response to US flying long-range bombers over South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Thursday threatened unspecified retaliation after the U.S. flew long-range bombers over South Korea during training with South Korean warplanes, which North Korea views as practice for an attack against it. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli defense minister says troops will remain in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s defense minister said on Wednesday that troops will remain in so-called security zones in the Gaza Strip, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely, remarks that could further complicate talks with Hamas over a ceasefire and hostage release.
item.dc:creator: ISAAC SCHARF, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A Berlin doctor has been charged with the killings of 15 patients under palliative care
item.description: A German doctor has been charged with murdering over 15 people under palliative care; he has also been accused of attempting to cover up the crimes by starting fires in their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at recent global actions limiting legal recognitions for transgender people
item.description: A ruling Wednesday from the top court in the United Kingdom that says the legal definition of a woman is someone born biologically female is the latest high-profile action globally involving the issue of what legal recognitions transgender people are allowed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Trump officials will hold talks with Europeans on the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Steve Witkoff, President Donald Trump’s special envoy, will travel to Paris this week for talks with European allies on U.S. efforts to negotiate a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Maryland man's family questions death, says he didn't drown in Bahamas
item.description: Dinari McAlmont of Bowie, Maryland, was found dead April 5 near the Atlantis resort on Paradise Island, according to Royal Bahamian Police.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Hong Kong post office will stop shipping parcels to US over tariffs
item.description: The U.S. earlier announced it would end a customs exception allowing small-value parcels from Hong Kong to enter the U.S. without tax.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Father of American-Israeli hostage pushes for ceasefire
item.description: Adi Alexander shares a message for his son, urging him to stay strong and assuring him the family won’t stop fighting for his return.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9

item.title: Poland says Moscow is 'mocking' Trump with deadly Ukraine strike
item.description: Poland is urging the U.S. to respond to Russian President Vladimir Putin's "mocking" response to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure peace deal by striking civilians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin secures release of American missionary held in Tunisia for 13 months: report
item.description: American missionary Robert Vieira, detained in Tunisia for more than a year, is set to return home soon after officials secured his release on Sunday, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel intensifies strikes across Gaza on Palm Sunday and hits a hospital in the north
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza (AP) — A wave of Israeli strikes across Gaza on Sunday hit a hospital, a municipal building, a home and a vehicle, killing at least 21 people, including children, as Israel vowed to expand its security presence in the small coastal strip.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis makes appearance after Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still recovering from double pneumonia, but the 88-year-old still met with faithful after the Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican on April 13, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin makes new move to bring South African refugees to US as president blasts nation's rulers again
item.description: As the U.S. begins refugee processing for South African Afrikaners, a group is pushing Washington to support its bid for an autonomous Afrikaner homeland within South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
